title: _elementtree.XMLParser / pyexpat: concurrent feed()/flush()/close() on a shared parser race the single-threaded expat parser's internal state (Modules/expat/xmlparse.c)
description: xml.etree.ElementTree.XMLParser (and pyexpat.xmlparser) wrap a single libexpat parser, which is single-threaded by design. On a free-threaded build, calling the parser's methods concurrently on ONE shared parser races expat's internal state with no CPython-side lock: e.g. XMLParser.flush() -> PyExpat_XML_SetReparseDeferralEnabled (xmlparse.c:3055) writes parser state while XMLParser.close()/feed() -> expat_parse -> PyExpat_XML_Parse/XML_ParseBuffer -> callProcessor (xmlparse.c:1188) reads/advances it. TSan reports many faces across the expat parse path (XML_Parse self-race, XML_ParseBuffer|callProcessor, |doProlog, |errorProcessor, SetReparseDeferralEnabled|callProcessor).
sites: Modules/expat/xmlparse.c PyExpat_XML_Parse / XML_ParseBuffer / callProcessor / doProlog (advance the shared expat parser's state machine)
CPython wrappers: Modules/_elementtree.c expat_parse / _elementtree_XMLParser_close_impl / _flush_impl (no critical section around the expat calls)
race_kind: shared single-threaded C library object: concurrent use of one libexpat parser races its internal state. CPython's XMLParser/pyexpat wrapper takes no per-parser lock around the expat calls (same class as the thread-unsafe-libc localeconv, TSAN-0047 / cpython#127081).
shared_state: the libexpat XML_Parser struct (parse position, buffer, processor pointers, reparse-deferral flag) inside a shared _elementtree.XMLParser / pyexpat.xmlparser
severity: low-to-moderate: expat's internal state is corrupted under concurrent use (mis-parse, or reads of half-updated processor pointers). Sharing one parser across threads and driving it concurrently is unusual/misuse (a parser is inherently sequential), which caps priority. Free-threaded build only.
repro_reliability: deterministic: repro.py (8 threads sharing one ET.XMLParser, roles feed()/flush()/close()) exits 66 on debug-ft-nojit-tsan (SUMMARY: PyExpat_XML_Parse self-race). Magalu fleet drove the flush-vs-close faces (SetReparseDeferralEnabled|callProcessor etc.).
suggested_fix: Take a per-parser critical section (Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(self)) around the expat calls in the XMLParser/pyexpat methods (feed/flush/close/Parse), so one shared parser is driven serially -- OR document that a parser must not be shared across threads (it is inherently sequential). Related to the broader _elementtree FT hardening (gh-149816 / gh-146022).
prior_art: gh api search found no specific issue for the XMLParser/expat shared-parser race. Same shared-thread-unsafe-C-object class as localeconv (TSAN-0047 / cpython#127081). The _elementtree FT effort (gh-149816 / gh-146022) is adjacent but about Element, not the parser.
confirmed_build: 3.16_ft_debug_tsan (magalu remote); reproduced independently on our debug-ft-nojit-tsan
related: Shared-single-threaded-C-object class (cf. localeconv TSAN-0047). Distinct from the _elementtree Element `extra` race (TSAN-0041) and TreeBuilder (TSAN-0031/0022). Surfaced in a downloaded remote fleet.
notes: NEW / appears unfiled, but LOW priority: sharing one XML parser across threads and driving it concurrently is misuse (a parser is sequential by nature). Fileable only if CPython wants XMLParser/pyexpat to lock. Cataloged for dedup. Not proposing a filing without maintainer interest.
